checks.command: compliance.yml: 2 SHA-pinned uses: lines with version comments
behavioral: C5 missing-target (NIZAM.json deleted, mktemp scratch) emits [C5] FAIL and still reaches SUMMARY
behavioral: fixture-scoped comment tolerance -- non-fixture leading-comment doc fails C1, existing fixtures still pass
product_spec_002.md: Sec 3 tree has tools/fixtures/, frontmatter version/spec_version 1.0.1 + matching change_log
clean-tree validate.sh: exact 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed', exit 0
scope guard: non-.agent diff exactly compliance.yml+validate.sh; .agent diff limited to product_spec_002.md/contract/evidence/run_state
equivalence guard: workflow run step is still exactly 'bash tools/validate.sh'

adversarial_check.description: Three-part mandatory check beyond the 8 listed commands. (a) External Anchor: queried the live GitHub API directly (not run_state's recorded provenance) for both tags -- api.github.com/repos/actions/checkout/git/refs/tags/v4.2.2 resolves to 11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683, and api.github.com/repos/actions/setup-python/git/refs/tags/v5.3.0 resolves to 0b93645e9fea7318ecaed2b359559ac225c90a2b -- both match the workflow's pinned SHAs exactly, independently reconfirming the provenance this contract consumed rather than trusting it. (b) Full structural YAML parse of the complete final workflow shape: triggers (pull_request unfiltered, push branches==[main]), permissions=={'contents':'read'}, concurrency group+cancel-in-progress, exactly 1 job/4 steps, both pinned uses: lines with their full with-blocks (fetch-depth:0 + persist-credentials:false; python-version:3.x), the pip-install step, and the run step -- all present, nothing silently dropped in the hardening edit. (c) git-stash-isolated comparison of tools/validate.sh's clean-tree summary immediately before vs. after this feature's three file changes: both sides produce byte-identical 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed' with exit 0 -- no check-count drift. Working tree confirmed restored and stash-clean afterward.
